title: pteris cretica subsp. cretica
intro: Pteris cretica subsp. cretica, also known as the bracken fern or the resurrection fern, is a species of fern that is native to tropical and subtropical regions around the world. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which have been attributed to the presence of various compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Pteris cretica subsp. cretica have been linked to several health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and acting as an antioxidant. Herbal preparations of this plant, such as teas and infusions, are often used in traditional medicine, although further research is needed to fully understand its potential applications and effects.
